statement: A RunPod spec guide cites the NVIDIA B200 (SXM) at 20 petaFLOPS FP4 per GPU with a stated 700W TDP, but independent sources cite 1000W SXM TDP and up to 18 PFLOPS sparse FP4, so a reliable performance-per-watt figure cannot be computed.
why: Blackwell B200 raises per-GPU FP4 throughput over prior Hopper parts, implying better performance-per-watt, but the unresolved TDP conflict prevents a trustworthy number.

impact.mechanism: Higher per-GPU throughput at comparable power improves compute efficiency and eases power-constrained datacenter deployment.

confidence.basis: Secondary source with an explicit TDP data conflict (700W vs widely-cited 1000W SXM) that prevents a reliable perf/W calculation.

dispersion: TDP cited 700W vs independent 1000W; FP4 20 PFLOPS vs up to 18 PFLOPS sparse
